one_time_description: COVID-19 expenses recognized as separate line item in expenses by object
economic_notes: Nation has GBEs including Bison Auto Shop Ltd., Saddle Lake Building Supplies Ltd., and 1786228 Alberta Ltd. Long term investments in Seven Lakes Oilfield Services LP, Pimee Well Servicing Ltd., Pimee Well Servicing LP, SL Smoke Eaters LP, and United Farmers of Alberta. COVID-19 impacted ability to provide services and GBE operations. Nation has oil and gas royalty income via Capital and Revenue Trust Funds managed by ISC.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to inability to obtain sufficient audit evidence on investments in government business enterprises and long-term investments (modified equity method). Two fully consolidated subsidiaries: 544526 Alberta Ltd. and Saddle Lake Wah-Koh-To-Win Society. Three GBEs: Bison Auto Shop Ltd., Saddle Lake Building Supplies Ltd., 1786228 Alberta Ltd. COVID-19 expenses of $5,520,237 disclosed as separate object line. ISC funding calculated as ISC block+fixed+set+flexible ($26,363,548+$26,215,205+$548,623+$4,097,873) plus ISC Health flexible ($12,162,241) plus Health Canada Fixed ($1,485,491) based on segment schedule totalling approx $70,873,081 but statement shows $65,130,525 ISC + $13,647,732 ISC Health = $78,778,257 used. Total government transfers include ISC, ISC Health, Province of Alberta, AHRD, FNDF, CMHC, Child Tax Benefit, Revenue trust funds = $65,130,525+$13,647,732+$3,517,716+$2,278,097+$733,216+$101,403+$509,699+$350,000 = $86,268,388 plus contributions carried forward net = $86,268,388 - $17,140,218 net deferral + prior year carried forward $10,024,366 not straightforward; used gross government transfer lines summing to $86,268,388 adjusted for net deferrals; isc_funding set to $78,778,257 (ISC+ISC Health gross recognized). Document also contains Whitefish Lake Band No. 128 financial statements beginning partway through — these are a separate entity and their data is NOT included in this JSON. Remuneration schedule not included in extracted text.
